28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass

94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

468	IDENTITY_DROPOUT = torch.nn.Identity
469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result
543	pass

905	def install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False):
906	    # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
907	    # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??
908	    # use_cuda = "LLAMA_CUDA=1" if use_cuda else ""
909	
910	    commands = [
911	        "git clone --recursive https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp",
912	        "pip install gguf protobuf",
913	    ]
914	    if os.path.exists("llama.cpp"): return
915	    try_execute(commands)
916	
917	    commands = [
918	        "make clean -C llama.cpp",
919	        # https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/issues/7062
920	        # Weirdly GPU conversion for GGUF breaks??
921	        # f"{use_cuda} make all -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} -C llama.cpp",
922	        f"make all -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} -C llama.cpp",
923	    ]
924	    if try_execute(commands) == "CMAKE":
925	        # Instead use CMAKE
926	        commands = [
927	            "cmake llama.cpp -B llama.cpp/build -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=ON",
928	            f"cmake --build llama.cpp/build --config Release -j{psutil.cpu_count()*2} --clean-first --target {' '.join(LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS)}",
929	            "cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp",
930	            "rm -rf llama.cpp/build",
931	        ]
932	        try_execute(commands)
933	    pass
934	pass

937	def get_executable(executables):
938	    # Get system locations (System Path).split(system separator)
939	    system_directories = os.environ.get("PATH").split(os.pathsep)
940	
941	    for directory in system_directories:
942	        for executable in executables:
943	            path = os.path.join(directory, executable)
944	            # Check if the executable exists and is executable
945	            if os.path.exists(path) and os.access(path, os.X_OK): return path
946	        pass
947	    pass
948	    return None
949	pass

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3
1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'
1031	    print(print_info)
1032	
1033	    # Check first_conversion format

161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():

180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)

205	def unsloth_save_model(
206	    model,
207	    tokenizer,
208	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
209	    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
210	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
211	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
212	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
213	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
214	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
215	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
216	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
217	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
218	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
219	
220	    # Push to hub
221	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
222	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
223	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
224	    create_pr            : bool = False,
225	    revision             : str = None,
226	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
227	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
228	
229	    # Our functions
230	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
231	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
232	):
233	    if token is None: token = get_token()
234	
235	    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
236	    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
237	        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
238	    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()
239	
240	    if commit_description is None:
241	        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
242	    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
243	        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
244	    pass
245	
246	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
247	        raise RuntimeError(
248	            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
249	            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
250	            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
251	            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
252	        )
253	    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
254	        save_method = "merged_4bit"
255	    pass
256	
257	    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
258	    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
259	        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!
263	    if push_to_hub:
264	        from huggingface_hub import whoami
265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
267	        except:
268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
270	                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
284	    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
287	            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
288	            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
289	            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
290	        )
291	    pass
292	
293	    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
294	
295	        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
296	        print("This might take 5 minutes...")
297	
298	        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
299	        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
300	            model = model.merge_and_unload()
301	        pass
302	        print("Done.")
303	    pass

2502	def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
2503	    self,
2504	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2505	    tokenizer            = None,
2506	    torchao_config       = None,
2507	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2508	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2509	):
2510	    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint
2511	
2512	    Args
2513	      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
2515	      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
2516	    """
2517	    # first merge the lora weights
2518	    arguments = dict(locals())
2519	    arguments["model"]        = self
2520	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
2521	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
2522	    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2523	    del arguments["self"]
2524	    del arguments["torchao_config"]
2525	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2526	    for _ in range(3):
2527	        gc.collect()
2528	
2529	    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
2530	    from torchao import quantize_
2531	    if torchao_config is None:
2532	        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
2533	        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
2534	    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)
2535	
2536	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
2537	    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
2538	        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
2539	    else:
2540	        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
2541	    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
2542	        arguments["save_directory"],
2543	        device_map = "auto",
2544	        quantization_config = quantization_config,
2545	        **kwargs,
2546	    )
2547	
2548	    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"
2549	
2550	    if push_to_hub:
2551	        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
2552	        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
2553	        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
2554	        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
2555	    else:
2556	        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
2557	        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
2558	    pass
2559	    for _ in range(3):
2560	        gc.collect()
2561	pass

1951	def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
1952	    self,
1953	    repo_id              : str,
1954	    tokenizer            = None,
1955	    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
1956	    first_conversion     : str = None,
1957	    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
1958	    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
1959	    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
1960	    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
1961	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
1962	    create_pr            : bool = False,
1963	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
1964	    revision             : str = None,
1965	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
1966	    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
1967	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1968	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1969	):
1970	    """
1971	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1972	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1973	
1974	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1975	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1976	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1977	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1978	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1979	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1980	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",

77	def _create_unsloth_optimizer(
78	    model,
79	    optimizer_cls,
80	    optimizer_kwargs,
81	    embedding_lr = 5e-5,
82	):
83	    lr = optimizer_kwargs["lr"]
84	    weight_decay = optimizer_kwargs.get("weight_decay", 0.0)
85	
86	    param_groups = \
87	    {
88	        "non_embeddings" : {},
89	        "embeddings"     : {},
90	    }
91	
92	    for name, param in model.named_parameters():
93	        if not param.requires_grad: continue
94	        if name.endswith("modules_to_save.default.weight"):
95	            partial_name = name[:-len(".modules_to_save.default.weight")]
96	            partial_name = partial_name[partial_name.rfind(".")+1:]
97	            print(f"Unsloth: Setting lr = {embedding_lr:.2e} instead of {lr:.2e} for {partial_name}.")
98	            param_groups["embeddings"]    [name] = param
99	        else:
100	            param_groups["non_embeddings"][name] = param
101	        pass
102	    pass
103	
104	    optimizer_grouped_parameters = [
105	        {
106	            "params"       : list(param_groups["non_embeddings"].values()),
107	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
108	            "lr"           : lr,
109	        },
110	        {
111	            "params"       : list(param_groups["embeddings"].values()),
112	            "weight_decay" : weight_decay,
113	            "lr"           : embedding_lr,
114	        },
115	    ]
116	    optimizer = optimizer_cls(optimizer_grouped_parameters, **optimizer_kwargs)
117	    return optimizer
118	pass

121	class UnslothTrainer(SFTTrainer):
122	    def create_optimizer(self):
123	        embedding_learning_rate = getattr(self.args, "embedding_learning_rate", None)
124	        if embedding_learning_rate is None: return super().create_optimizer()
125	
126	        if self.optimizer is None:
127	            optimizer_cls, optimizer_kwargs = SFTTrainer.get_optimizer_cls_and_kwargs(self.args)
128	            self.optimizer = _create_unsloth_optimizer(
129	                self.model,
130	                optimizer_cls,
131	                optimizer_kwargs,
132	                embedding_learning_rate,
133	            )
134	        pass
135	        return self.optimizer
136	    pass

139	# From `trl>=0.13.0`, they changed how to pass several params to the trainer
140	# We need to patch to make the transition smooth
141	def _backwards_compatible_trainer(trainer_class, config_class):
142	    original_init = trainer_class.__init__
143	    
144	    @wraps(original_init)
145	    def new_init(self, *args, **kwargs):
146	        # All Trainer tokenizer are now called processing_class
147	        trainer_params = set(inspect.signature(original_init).parameters.keys())
148	
149	        if "processing_class" in trainer_params and "tokenizer" in kwargs:
150	            kwargs["processing_class"] = kwargs.pop("tokenizer")
151	        pass
152	
153	        if ("args" in kwargs) and (Version(trl.__version__) >= Version("0.13.0.dev0")):
154	            training_args = kwargs.pop("args", None)
155	
156	            # Get parameters that Trainer.__init__ actually expects
157	            trainer_params.remove('self')
158	            trainer_params.remove('args')
159	
160	            # Get fields that should be passed to Config init
161	            config_fields = {
162	                field.name: field for field in dataclasses.fields(config_class) 
163	                if field.init
164	            }
165	            
166	            # Create config dict with valid fields from training_args
167	            config_dict = {
168	                name: getattr(training_args, name)
169	                for name in config_fields
170	                if hasattr(training_args, name)
171	            }
172	
173	            # Get parameters that exist in Config but not in TrainingArguments
174	            from transformers import TrainingArguments
175	            moved_params = \
176	                set(inspect.signature(config_class)     .parameters.keys()) - \
177	                set(inspect.signature(TrainingArguments).parameters.keys())
178	            
179	            # Separate kwargs into trainer kwargs and config kwargs
180	            trainer_kwargs = {}
181	            additional_config_kwargs = {}
182	
183	            for key, value in kwargs.items():
184	                if key in trainer_params: trainer_kwargs[key] = value
185	                elif key in moved_params or key in config_fields:
186	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
187	                else:
188	                    additional_config_kwargs[key] = value
189	                pass
190	            pass
191	
192	            # Update config_dict with additional kwargs
193	            config_dict.update(additional_config_kwargs)
194	
195	            # Create Config with all the collected parameters
196	            # Reinitialising config class with parameters (that were none initially but populated on first init)
197	            # causes the 2nd init to fail as there are mutual exclusive checks on pairs of parameters.
198	            # Refer: https://github.com/huggingface/trl/blob/main/trl/trainer/grpo_config.py#L499-L502 for example
199	            # So we only create config class if the previous init was not TrainingArguments
200	            if not isinstance(training_args, TrainingArguments):
201	                config = config_class(**config_dict)
202	            else:
203	                config = training_args
204	
205	            # Reconstruct kwargs for Trainer
206	            kwargs = trainer_kwargs
207	            kwargs["args"] = config
208	        pass
209	        original_init(self, *args, **kwargs)
210	    pass
211	    return new_init
212	pass
213	
214	
215	def _patch_trl_trainer():
216	    import trl
217	    if hasattr(trl, "__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__"): return
218	    if Version(trl.__version__) <= Version("0.11.0"): return
219	
220	    import trl.trainer
221	    trl_classes = dir(trl.trainer)
222	    trl_trainers = set(x[:-len("Trainer")] for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Trainer"))
223	    trl_configs  = set(x[:-len("Config")]  for x in trl_classes if x.endswith("Config"))
224	    trl_classes = list(trl_trainers & trl_configs)
225	
226	    for x in trl_classes:
227	        try:    exec(f"trl.{x}Trainer.__init__ = _backwards_compatible_trainer(trl.{x}Trainer, trl.{x}Config)", globals())
228	        except: continue
229	    pass
230	
231	    trl.__UNSLOTH_BACKWARDS_COMPATIBLE__ = True
232	pass

2	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
3	from enum import Enum
4	
5	
6	class QuantType(Enum):
7	    BNB = "bnb"
8	    UNSLOTH = "unsloth" # dynamic 4-bit quantization
9	    GGUF = "GGUF"
10	    NONE = "none"
11	    BF16 = "bf16" # only for Deepseek V3
12	
13	# Tags for Hugging Face model paths
14	BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG = "bnb-4bit"
15	UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG = "unsloth" + "-" + BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG
16	GGUF_TAG = "GGUF"
17	BF16_TAG = "bf16"
18	
19	QUANT_TAG_MAP = {
20	    QuantType.BNB: BNB_QUANTIZED_TAG,
21	    QuantType.UNSLOTH: UNSLOTH_DYNAMIC_QUANT_TAG,
22	    QuantType.GGUF: GGUF_TAG,
23	    QuantType.NONE: None,
24	    QuantType.BF16: BF16_TAG,
25	} 
26	
27	# NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
28	@dataclass
29	class ModelInfo:
30	    org: str
31	    base_name: str
32	    version: str
33	    size: int
34	    name: str = None  # full model name, constructed from base_name, version, and size unless provided
35	    is_multimodal: bool = False
36	    instruct_tag: str = None
37	    quant_type: QuantType = None
38	    description: str = None
39	
40	    def __post_init__(self):
41	        self.name = self.name or self.construct_model_name(
42	            self.base_name,
43	            self.version,
44	            self.size,
45	            self.quant_type,
46	            self.instruct_tag,
47	        )
48	
49	    @staticmethod
50	    def append_instruct_tag(key: str, instruct_tag: str = None):
51	        if instruct_tag:
52	            key = "-".join([key, instruct_tag])
53	        return key
54	
55	    @staticmethod
56	    def append_quant_type(
57	        key: str, quant_type: QuantType = None
58	    ):
59	        if quant_type != QuantType.NONE:
60	            key = "-".join([key, QUANT_TAG_MAP[quant_type]])
61	        return key
62	
63	    @classmethod
64	    def construct_model_name(cls, base_name, version, size, quant_type, instruct_tag, key=""):
65	        key = cls.append_instruct_tag(key, instruct_tag)
66	        key = cls.append_quant_type(key, quant_type)
67	        return key
68	
69	    @property
70	    def model_path(
71	        self,
72	    ) -> str:
73	        return f"{self.org}/{self.name}"
74	
75	
76	@dataclass
77	class ModelMeta:
78	    org: str
79	    base_name: str
80	    model_version: str
81	    model_info_cls: type[ModelInfo]
82	    model_sizes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
83	    instruct_tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
84	    quant_types: list[QuantType] | dict[str, list[QuantType]] = field(default_factory=list)
85	    is_multimodal: bool = False
86	
87	
88	MODEL_REGISTRY: dict[str, ModelInfo] = {}
89	
90	
91	def register_model(
92	    model_info_cls: ModelInfo,
93	    org: str,
94	    base_name: str,
95	    version: str,
96	    size: int,
97	    instruct_tag: str = None,
98	    quant_type: QuantType = None,
99	    is_multimodal: bool = False,
100	    name: str = None,
101	):
102	    name = name or model_info_cls.construct_model_name(
103	        base_name=base_name,
104	        version=version,
105	        size=size,
106	        quant_type=quant_type,
107	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
108	    )
109	    key = f"{org}/{name}"
110	
111	    if key in MODEL_REGISTRY:
112	        raise ValueError(f"Model {key} already registered, current keys: {MODEL_REGISTRY.keys()}")
113	
114	    MODEL_REGISTRY[key] = model_info_cls(
115	        org=org,
116	        base_name=base_name,
117	        version=version,
118	        size=size,
119	        is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
120	        instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
121	        quant_type=quant_type,
122	        name=name,
123	    )

126	def _check_model_info(model_id: str, properties: list[str] = ["lastModified"]):
127	    from huggingface_hub import HfApi
128	    from huggingface_hub import ModelInfo as HfModelInfo
129	    from huggingface_hub.utils import RepositoryNotFoundError
130	
131	    api = HfApi()
132	
133	    try:
134	        model_info: HfModelInfo = api.model_info(model_id, expand=properties)
135	    except Exception as e:
136	        if isinstance(e, RepositoryNotFoundError):
137	            warnings.warn(f"{model_id} not found on Hugging Face")
138	            model_info = None
139	        else:
140	            raise e
141	    return model_info

144	def _register_models(model_meta: ModelMeta, include_original_model: bool = False):
145	    org = model_meta.org
146	    base_name = model_meta.base_name
147	    instruct_tags = model_meta.instruct_tags
148	    model_version = model_meta.model_version
149	    model_sizes = model_meta.model_sizes
150	    is_multimodal = model_meta.is_multimodal
151	    quant_types = model_meta.quant_types
152	    model_info_cls = model_meta.model_info_cls
153	
154	    for size in model_sizes:
155	        for instruct_tag in instruct_tags:
156	            # Handle quant types per model size
157	            if isinstance(quant_types, dict):
158	                _quant_types = quant_types[size]
159	            else:
160	                _quant_types = quant_types
161	            for quant_type in _quant_types:
162	                # NOTE: models registered with org="unsloth" and QUANT_TYPE.NONE are aliases of QUANT_TYPE.UNSLOTH
163	                _org = "unsloth" # unsloth models -- these are all quantized versions of the original model
164	                register_model(
165	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
166	                    org=_org,
167	                    base_name=base_name,
168	                    version=model_version,
169	                    size=size,
170	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
171	                    quant_type=quant_type,
172	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
173	                )
174	            # include original model from releasing organization
175	            if include_original_model:
176	                register_model(
177	                    model_info_cls=model_info_cls,
178	                    org=org,
179	                    base_name=base_name,
180	                    version=model_version,
181	                    size=size,
182	                    instruct_tag=instruct_tag,
183	                    quant_type=QuantType.NONE,
184	                    is_multimodal=is_multimodal,
185	                )

814	def try_execute(commands, force_complete = False):
815	    for command in commands:
816	        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell = True, stdout = subprocess.PIPE, stderr = subprocess.STDOUT, bufsize = 1) as sp:
817	            for line in sp.stdout:
818	                line = line.decode("utf-8", errors = "replace")
819	                if "undefined reference" in line:
820	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
821	                elif "deprecated" in line:
822	                    return "CMAKE"
823	                elif "Unknown argument" in line:
824	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
825	                elif "***" in line:
826	                    raise RuntimeError(f"*** Unsloth: Failed compiling llama.cpp with {line}. Please report this ASAP!")
827	                print(line, flush = True, end = "")
828	            pass
829	            if force_complete and sp.returncode is not None and sp.returncode != 0:
830	                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, sp.args)
831	        pass
832	    pass
833	    return None

838	    # Download the 10th latest release since the latest might be broken!
839	    # FALLBACK mechanism
840	    releases = subprocess.check_output(["git", "ls-remote", "--tags", "https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp.git"])
841	    releases = releases.decode("utf-8").replace("\t", " ").split("\n")
842	    for i, x in enumerate(releases):
843	        if "refs/tags/b" not in x: break
844	    releases = releases[:i]
845	    latest = releases[-1]
846	    version = releases[version].split(" ")[0]
847	
848	    # Check if the llama.cpp exists

850	        print(

854	        )

3	"""
4	🦥 Starter Script for Fine-Tuning FastLanguageModel with Unsloth
5	
6	This script is designed as a starting point for fine-tuning your models using unsloth.
7	It includes configurable options for model loading, PEFT parameters, training arguments, 
8	and model saving/pushing functionalities.

10	You will likely want to customize this script to suit your specific use case 
11	and requirements.
12	
13	Here are a few suggestions for customization:
14	    - Modify the dataset loading and preprocessing steps to match your data.
15	    - Customize the model saving and pushing configurations.
16	
17	Usage: (most of the options have valid default values this is an extended example for demonstration purposes)
18	    python unsloth-cli.py --model_name "unsloth/llama-3-8b" --max_seq_length 8192 --dtype None --load_in_4bit \
19	    --r 64 --lora_alpha 32 --lora_dropout 0.1 --bias "none" --use_gradient_checkpointing "unsloth" \
20	    --random_state 3407 --use_rslora --per_device_train_batch_size 4 --gradient_accumulation_steps 8 \
21	    --warmup_steps 5 --max_steps 400 --learning_rate 2e-6 --logging_steps 1 --optim "adamw_8bit" \
22	    --weight_decay 0.005 --lr_scheduler_type "linear" --seed 3407 --output_dir "outputs" \
23	    --report_to "tensorboard" --save_model --save_path "model" --quantization_method "f16" \
24	    --push_model --hub_path "hf/model" --hub_token "your_hf_token"

26	To see a full list of configurable options, use:

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report on all the save and export methods found in the unsloth codebase:

## Comprehensive Summary: Unsloth Save and Export Methods

Based on my thorough search of the unsloth codebase, here are all the existing save and export methods:

### **1. GGUF Export Implementation**

**Primary Function:** `save_to_gguf()` at line 952
- **Location:** `/unsloth/save.py:952-1050+`
- **Signature:**
```python
def save_to_gguf(
    model_type           : str,
    model_dtype          : str,
    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
)
```

**Key Features:**
- Supports multiple quantization methods: `not_quantized`, `fast_quantized`, `quantized`, `f32`, `f16`, `q8_0`, `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q2_k`, `q3_k_l`, `q3_k_m`, `q3_k_s`, `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q4_k_s`, `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q5_k_s`, `q6_k`
- Installs llama.cpp automatically (non-blocking)
- Uses llama.cpp's convert-hf-to-gguf.py script for conversion
- Quantizes using llama-quantize command
- Returns `all_file_locations` and `want_full_precision` flag

**High-level GGUF Flow:**
1. Validates quantization methods
2. Installs llama.cpp (if not exists)
3. Converts HF model to GGUF 16-bit format
4. Applies quantization to each specified type
5. Fixes SentencePiece tokenizers if needed

---

**Method 1: `save_pretrained_gguf()` at line 1768 (Language Models)**
```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
)
```

**Logic Flow (lines 1819-1950):**
1. Validates tokenizer exists (required for GGUF)
2. Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"` to merge LoRA
3. Installs llama.cpp dependencies (gguf, protobuf)
4. Extracts model dtype and type from config
5. Calls `save_to_gguf()` to perform actual conversion
6. Creates Ollama Modelfile if applicable
7. Uploads GGUF files to HuggingFace Hub (if `push_to_hub=True`)

---

**Method 2: `push_to_hub_gguf()` at line 1951 (Language Models)**
```python
def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
)
```

**Logic (lines 1995-2110+):**
- Similar to `save_pretrained_gguf()` but pushes to HubFace instead of saving locally
- Uses the same `save_to_gguf()` backend
- Handles organization uploads by manually uploading files with HfApi

---

**Method 3: `save_to_gguf_generic()` at line 2276 (Vision Models)**
```python
@torch.inference_mode
def save_to_gguf_generic(
    model,
    save_directory,
    tokenizer,
    quantization_method = None,
    quantization_type = "Q8_0",
    repo_id = None,
    token = None,
)
```

**Logic:**
- Installs llama.cpp if needed
- Uses unsloth_zoo's `_convert_to_gguf()` for conversion
- Supports uploading via HfApi

---

### **2. Merged Model Saving**

**Core Function:** `unsloth_save_model()` at line 205
```python
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
)
```

**Supports Three Save Methods (lines 283-291):**

1. **`"lora"`** - Saves only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
2. **`"merged_16bit"`** - Merges LoRA weights into float16 (needed for GGUF/llama.cpp)
3. **`"merged_4bit"`** - Merges LoRA into 4-bit weights (for DPO/HF inference)

**Merged 16-bit Flow (lines 483-759):**

1. Calls `_merge_lora()` for each LLAMA weight layer (lines 567-602)
2. Merges weights using the formula: `W_merged = W_base + s * A.T @ B.T`
3. Manages memory using GPU/CPU/disk based on VRAM usage
4. Handles sharded saving for large models
5. Removes quantization_config from model config
6. Saves via `save_pretrained()`

**Key Helper: `_merge_lora()` at line 159**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # Validate merge succeeded
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

**Logic:**
- Extracts LoRA weights (A, B) and scaling factor (s)
- Dequantizes 4-bit weights if needed
- Performs merge: `W += s * A.T @ B.T` using `addmm_()` (in-place fused operation)
- Validates for numerical stability (checks for infinity)
- Returns merged weight and bias

```python
)
```


**Supports Three Save Methods (lines 283-291):**

1. **`"lora"`** - Saves only LoRA adapters (fastest, smallest)
2. **`"merged_16bit"`** - Merges LoRA weights into float16 (needed for GGUF/llama.cpp)
3. **`"merged_4bit"`** - Merges LoRA into 4-bit weights (for DPO/HF inference)

**Merged 16-bit Flow (lines 483-759):**

1. Calls `_merge_lora()` for each LLAMA weight layer (lines 567-602)
2. Merges weights using the formula: `W_merged = W_base + s * A.T @ B.T`
3. Manages memory using GPU/CPU/disk based on VRAM usage
4. Handles sharded saving for large models
5. Removes quantization_config from model config

**Key Helper: `_merge_lora()` at line 159**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        
        if A is not None:
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # Validate merge succeeded
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
```

**Logic:**
- Extracts LoRA weights (A, B) and scaling factor (s)
- Dequantizes 4-bit weights if needed
- Performs merge: `W += s * A.T @ B.T` using `addmm_()` (in-place fused operation)
- Validates for numerical stability (checks for infinity)
- Returns merged weight and bias

## LoRA Adapter Weight Handling in Unsloth - Complete Analysis

Based on my thorough search of the unsloth codebase, here's a comprehensive explanation of how LoRA adapter weights are handled:

### 1. LoRA A and B Matrices: Storage and Access

**Storage Structure:**
- LoRA A and B matrices are stored as module attributes in PEFT layer wrappers
- They are accessed via dictionary indexing by adapter name: `layer.lora_A[adapter_name]` and `layer.lora_B[adapter_name]`
- Each returns a linear module containing the weight matrix

**Files:**

## UNSLOTH CODEBASE EXPLORATION - COMPREHENSIVE REPORT

### 1. OVERALL DIRECTORY LAYOUT & MAIN PACKAGES

The unsloth repository is organized with the following top-level structure:

**Root Directory:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395142187-is5uuq/`

**Main Packages/Modules:**
- `unsloth/` - Core package directory
  - `__init__.py` - Main entry point exporting all public APIs
  - `save.py` (105KB) - Model saving/exporting functionality
  - `trainer.py` - Training-related utilities and patches
  - `chat_templates.py` (129KB) - Chat template definitions
  - `tokenizer_utils.py` (41KB) - Tokenizer utilities
  - `import_fixes.py` - Import compatibility fixes
  - `_auto_install.py` - Auto-installation utilities
  
  - `models/` - Model implementations (core functionality)
  - `kernels/` - CUDA/computation kernels
  - `registry/` - Model registry system
  - `dataprep/` - Data preparation utilities
  - `utils/` - General utilities

**Configuration & Entry Points:**
- `pyproject.toml` - Project metadata and dependencies
- `unsloth-cli.py` - CLI interface for fine-tuning workflows

---

### 2. MODEL SAVING/EXPORTING FUNCTIONALITY LOCATION

The entire model saving and exporting pipeline is centralized in:
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395142187-is5uuq/unsloth/save.py`

**Key Functions in save.py (2677 lines):**

1. **`unsloth_save_model()`** (Lines 205-763)
   - Main entry point for saving models
   - Supports save methods: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
   - Handles push_to_hub functionality
   - Manages memory efficiently during saves
   - Optional HuggingFace Hub integration

2. **`save_to_gguf()`** (Lines 952-1320)
   - Converts merged models to GGUF format
   - Supports multiple quantization methods (q4_k_m, q8_0, q5_k_m, f16, bf16, f32, etc.)
   - Integrates with llama.cpp for conversion
   - Handles both SentencePiece and BPE tokenizers

3. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`** (Lines 1768-1950)
   - Wrapper for saving directly to GGUF format
   - Merges LoRA adapters before GGUF conversion

4. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()`** (Lines 1951-2128)
   - Saves to GGUF and pushes to HuggingFace Hub

5. **`save_lora_to_custom_dir()`** (Lines 2129-2142)
   - Saves only LoRA adapter weights

6. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`** (Lines 2211-2275)
   - Converts LoRA to GGML format locally

7. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`** (Lines 2143-2210)
   - Converts LoRA to GGML and pushes to Hub

8. **`unsloth_generic_save()`** (Lines 2356-2412)
   - Generic save function for merged models

9. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`** (Lines 2502-2561)
   - Saves with TorchAO quantization

10. **`patch_saving_functions()`** (Lines 2569-2678)
    - Patches model objects with saving methods
    - Adds these methods to models:
      - `push_to_hub_merged`
      - `save_pretrained_merged`
      - `push_to_hub_gguf`
      - `save_pretrained_gguf`
      - `save_pretrained_torchao`
      - `push_to_hub_ggml`
      - `save_pretrained_ggml`

**Supporting Infrastructure Functions:**
- `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` - Detects tokenizer type
- `_free_cached_model()` - Manages HuggingFace cache
- `_merge_lora()` - Merges LoRA weights
- `install_llama_cpp_blocking()` - Installs llama.cpp for GGUF conversion
- `get_executable()` - Locates system executables
- `create_huggingface_repo()` - Creates HF repos
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - Manages HF uploads
- `create_ollama_modelfile()` - Creates Ollama model files
- `push_to_ollama()` - Pushes models to Ollama Hub

**Quantization Methods Supported:**
File defines `ALLOWED_QUANTS` dictionary with 23+ quantization types including:
- f32, bf16, f16 (full precision)
- q4_k_m, q5_k_m (recommended)
- q8_0 (fast)
- q2_k through q6_k variants
- q3_k_s/m/l, q4_k_s/m, q5_k_s variants

---

### 3. KEY ENTRY POINTS AND MODULE ORGANIZATION

**A. Main Public API (from `__init__.py`):**
```python
from .models import *
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *
```

**B. Models Module Organization:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/models/`

Key files and classes:
- `loader.py` - FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel

- `granite.py` - Granite models
- `falcon_h1.py` - Falcon H1 models
- `cohere.py` - Cohere models
- `vision.py` - Vision/multimodal model support
- `rl.py` - Reinforcement Learning models
- `_utils.py` - Shared utilities
- `mapper.py` - Layer mapping utilities
- `dpo.py` - DPO trainer patch
- `loader_utils.py` - Loader helper functions

**C. Model Registration System:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/registry/`
- `registry.py` - Model registry with quantization type tracking
- Model definitions:
  - `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_gemma.py`, `_qwen.py`, `_phi.py`, `_deepseek.py`
  - Tracks: org, base_name, version, size, quantization types, multimodal support

**D. Training Infrastructure:**
**File:** `/unsloth/trainer.py`
- `UnslothTrainingArguments` - Extended training config
- `UnslothTrainer` - SFTTrainer subclass
- `unsloth_train()` - Training wrapper
- `_patch_trl_trainer()` - TRL backward compatibility patches

**E. Kernels/Computation:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/kernels/`
- `fast_lora.py` - Optimized LoRA kernels
- `cross_entropy_loss.py` - Custom loss implementations
- `layernorm.py`, `rms_layernorm.py` - Optimized layer norms
- `rope_embedding.py` - RoPE implementation

- `moe/` - Mixture of Experts implementations
- `utils.py` - Kernel utilities

**F. Data Preparation:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/dataprep/`
- `synthetic.py` - Synthetic data generation
- `synthetic_configs.py` - Config for synthetic data

**G. CLI Entry Point:**
**File:** `unsloth-cli.py`
- Argument parser with sections:
  - Model Options
  - LoRA Options
  - Training Options
  - Report/Logging Options
  - Save Model Options (local and GGUF)
  - Push Model Options (HuggingFace Hub)
- Demonstrates complete workflow: load → configure → train → save/push

---

### 4. ADAPTER & LORA MANAGEMENT

**How LoRA Adapters are Managed:**

1. **LoRA Configuration:**
   - Adapters are attached via PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning) library
   - Unsloth provides `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()` wrapper
   - Supports target modules: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj

2. **Saving LoRA:**
   - Default `save_method="lora"` saves only adapter weights
   - Uses PEFT's native serialization
   - Much smaller file size than merged models
   - Can be loaded via `PeftModel.from_pretrained()`

3. **Merging & Conversion:**
   - `save_method="merged_16bit"` - Merges adapters into base model, saves as fp16
   - `save_method="merged_4bit"` - Merges and keeps 4-bit quantization
   - Merged models can be converted to GGUF
   - LoRA can be exported to GGML format via `push_to_hub_ggml` / `save_pretrained_ggml`

4. **Related Files:**
   - Fast LoRA kernels: `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (18KB)
   - No dedicated adapter directory - managed through PEFT integration

---

### 5. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE - DETAILED 2-3 LEVELS DEEP

```
unsloth/
├── __init__.py (11KB) - Main API exports
├── _auto_install.py - Auto installer
├── import_fixes.py (6KB) - Import compatibility
```

---

### 6. INTEGRATION POINTS - HOW SAVING GETS ATTACHED

The model saving methods are **dynamically patched** onto model instances:

**Process Flow:**
1. Model is created via `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()`
2. PEFT adapter is applied via `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()`
3. `patch_saving_functions()` from save.py is called
4. Methods are attached to model instance using `types.MethodType()`:
   - `model.save_pretrained_merged`
   - `model.push_to_hub_merged`
   - `model.save_pretrained_gguf`
   - `model.push_to_hub_gguf`
   - `model.save_pretrained_torchao`
   - `model.push_to_hub_ggml`
   - `model.save_pretrained_ggml`

**Vision vs Text Models:**
- Text models get full suite of save methods
- Vision models use simplified versions (GGUF not supported for vision)

---

### 7. KEY SEARCH TERMS FOUND IN CODEBASE

- **save_to_gguf** - GGUF conversion function
- **unsloth_save_model** - Main save entry point
- **patch_saving_functions** - Attaches save methods to models
- **save_pretrained_merged** - Merged model saving
- **push_to_hub_gguf** - GGUF Hub upload
- **save_lora_to_custom_dir** - LoRA-only save
- **quantization_method** - Q4, Q8, F16, BF16, etc.
- **ALLOWED_QUANTS** - 23+ quantization types
- **llama.cpp** - External GGUF conversion tool
- **PeftModel** - PEFT adapter base class
- **FastLanguageModel** - Main model loading class
- **FastVisionModel** - Multimodal model class

---

### 8. DEPENDENCIES & EXTERNAL INTEGRATIONS

**Key Dependencies (from pyproject.toml):**
- `transformers>=4.51.3` - Model loading
- `peft>=0.7.1` - Adapter management
- `trl>=0.7.9` - Training utilities
- `torch>=2.2` - Deep learning framework
- `bitsandbytes>=0.45.5` - Quantization
- `unsloth_zoo>=2025.9.11` - Zoo models and utilities
- `huggingface_hub>=0.34.0` - Hub integration
- `datasets>=3.4.1` - Data loading

**External Tools:**
- llama.cpp - For GGUF conversion and quantization
- HuggingFace Hub - For model sharing
- Ollama - For local model serving

---

### SUMMARY

Unsloth is a 2-5x faster LLM fine-tuning framework with a highly modular architecture:
- **Save/Export Logic**: Centralized in `save.py` with 25+ functions
- **Model Support**: 22 model architectures (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, etc.)
- **Save Formats**: LoRA, merged_16bit, merged_4bit, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO
- **Quantization**: 23+ quantization methods via llama.cpp
- **Integration**: HuggingFace Hub, Ollama, vLLM
- **Dynamic Patching**: Methods attached to models at runtime
- **Memory Efficient**: Handles large models through various optimization techniques

## UNSLOTH CODEBASE EXPLORATION - COMPREHENSIVE REPORT

### 1. OVERALL DIRECTORY LAYOUT & MAIN PACKAGES

The unsloth repository is organized with the following top-level structure:

**Root Directory:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395142187-is5uuq/`

**Main Packages/Modules:**
- `unsloth/` - Core package directory
  - `__init__.py` - Main entry point exporting all public APIs
  - `save.py` (105KB) - Model saving/exporting functionality
  - `trainer.py` - Training-related utilities and patches
  - `chat_templates.py` (129KB) - Chat template definitions
  - `tokenizer_utils.py` (41KB) - Tokenizer utilities
  - `import_fixes.py` - Import compatibility fixes
  - `_auto_install.py` - Auto-installation utilities
  
  - `models/` - Model implementations (core functionality)
  - `kernels/` - CUDA/computation kernels
  - `registry/` - Model registry system
  - `dataprep/` - Data preparation utilities
  - `utils/` - General utilities

**Configuration & Entry Points:**
- `pyproject.toml` - Project metadata and dependencies
- `unsloth-cli.py` - CLI interface for fine-tuning workflows

---

### 2. MODEL SAVING/EXPORTING FUNCTIONALITY LOCATION

The entire model saving and exporting pipeline is centralized in:
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775395142187-is5uuq/unsloth/save.py`

**Key Functions in save.py (2677 lines):**

1. **`unsloth_save_model()`** (Lines 205-763)
   - Main entry point for saving models
   - Supports save methods: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
   - Handles push_to_hub functionality
   - Manages memory efficiently during saves
   - Optional HuggingFace Hub integration

2. **`save_to_gguf()`** (Lines 952-1320)
   - Converts merged models to GGUF format
   - Supports multiple quantization methods (q4_k_m, q8_0, q5_k_m, f16, bf16, f32, etc.)
   - Integrates with llama.cpp for conversion
   - Handles both SentencePiece and BPE tokenizers

3. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`** (Lines 1768-1950)
   - Wrapper for saving directly to GGUF format
   - Merges LoRA adapters before GGUF conversion

4. **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()`** (Lines 1951-2128)
   - Saves to GGUF and pushes to HuggingFace Hub

5. **`save_lora_to_custom_dir()`** (Lines 2129-2142)
   - Saves only LoRA adapter weights

6. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()`** (Lines 2211-2275)
   - Converts LoRA to GGML format locally

7. **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`** (Lines 2143-2210)
   - Converts LoRA to GGML and pushes to Hub

8. **`unsloth_generic_save()`** (Lines 2356-2412)
   - Generic save function for merged models

9. **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`** (Lines 2502-2561)
   - Saves with TorchAO quantization

10. **`patch_saving_functions()`** (Lines 2569-2678)
    - Patches model objects with saving methods
    - Adds these methods to models:
      - `push_to_hub_merged`
      - `save_pretrained_merged`
      - `push_to_hub_gguf`
      - `save_pretrained_gguf`
      - `save_pretrained_torchao`
      - `push_to_hub_ggml`
      - `save_pretrained_ggml`

**Supporting Infrastructure Functions:**
- `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` - Detects tokenizer type
- `_free_cached_model()` - Manages HuggingFace cache
- `_merge_lora()` - Merges LoRA weights
- `install_llama_cpp_blocking()` - Installs llama.cpp for GGUF conversion
- `get_executable()` - Locates system executables
- `create_huggingface_repo()` - Creates HF repos
- `upload_to_huggingface()` - Manages HF uploads
- `create_ollama_modelfile()` - Creates Ollama model files
- `push_to_ollama()` - Pushes models to Ollama Hub

**Quantization Methods Supported:**
File defines `ALLOWED_QUANTS` dictionary with 23+ quantization types including:
- f32, bf16, f16 (full precision)
- q4_k_m, q5_k_m (recommended)
- q8_0 (fast)
- q2_k through q6_k variants
- q3_k_s/m/l, q4_k_s/m, q5_k_s variants

---

### 3. KEY ENTRY POINTS AND MODULE ORGANIZATION

**A. Main Public API (from `__init__.py`):**
```python
from .models import *
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *
```

**B. Models Module Organization:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/models/`

Key files and classes:
- `loader.py` - FastLanguageModel, FastVisionModel, FastTextModel, FastModel
- `llama.py` - FastLlamaModel
- `mistral.py` - FastMistralModel
- `gemma.py`, `gemma2.py` - Gemma model implementations
- `qwen2.py`, `qwen3.py`, `qwen3_moe.py` - Qwen implementations
- `granite.py` - Granite models
- `falcon_h1.py` - Falcon H1 models
- `cohere.py` - Cohere models
- `vision.py` - Vision/multimodal model support
- `rl.py` - Reinforcement Learning models
- `_utils.py` - Shared utilities
- `mapper.py` - Layer mapping utilities
- `dpo.py` - DPO trainer patch
- `loader_utils.py` - Loader helper functions

**C. Model Registration System:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/registry/`
- `registry.py` - Model registry with quantization type tracking
- Model definitions:
  - `_llama.py`, `_mistral.py`, `_gemma.py`, `_qwen.py`, `_phi.py`, `_deepseek.py`
  - Tracks: org, base_name, version, size, quantization types, multimodal support

**D. Training Infrastructure:**
**File:** `/unsloth/trainer.py`
- `UnslothTrainingArguments` - Extended training config
- `UnslothTrainer` - SFTTrainer subclass
- `unsloth_train()` - Training wrapper
- `_patch_trl_trainer()` - TRL backward compatibility patches

**E. Kernels/Computation:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/kernels/`
- `fast_lora.py` - Optimized LoRA kernels
- `cross_entropy_loss.py` - Custom loss implementations
- `layernorm.py`, `rms_layernorm.py` - Optimized layer norms
- `rope_embedding.py` - RoPE implementation
- `flex_attention.py`, `geglu.py`, `swiglu.py` - Activation functions
- `moe/` - Mixture of Experts implementations
- `utils.py` - Kernel utilities

**F. Data Preparation:**
**Directory:** `/unsloth/dataprep/`
- `synthetic.py` - Synthetic data generation
- `synthetic_configs.py` - Config for synthetic data

**G. CLI Entry Point:**
**File:** `unsloth-cli.py`
- Argument parser with sections:
  - Model Options
  - LoRA Options
  - Training Options
  - Report/Logging Options
  - Save Model Options (local and GGUF)
  - Push Model Options (HuggingFace Hub)
- Demonstrates complete workflow: load → configure → train → save/push

---

### 4. ADAPTER & LORA MANAGEMENT

**How LoRA Adapters are Managed:**

1. **LoRA Configuration:**
   - Adapters are attached via PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning) library
   - Unsloth provides `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()` wrapper
   - Supports target modules: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj

2. **Saving LoRA:**
   - Default `save_method="lora"` saves only adapter weights
   - Uses PEFT's native serialization
   - Much smaller file size than merged models
   - Can be loaded via `PeftModel.from_pretrained()`

3. **Merging & Conversion:**
   - `save_method="merged_16bit"` - Merges adapters into base model, saves as fp16
   - `save_method="merged_4bit"` - Merges and keeps 4-bit quantization
   - Merged models can be converted to GGUF
   - LoRA can be exported to GGML format via `push_to_hub_ggml` / `save_pretrained_ggml`

4. **Related Files:**
   - Fast LoRA kernels: `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (18KB)
   - No dedicated adapter directory - managed through PEFT integration

from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit

from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
import subprocess
import psutil
import re
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi
try:
    from huggingface_hub import get_token
except:
    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token
    except:
        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass
from pathlib import Path

def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass
